//: AEMaaCS OSGi config for the Granite Workflow Queue.
This is the real knob for workflow parallelism — NOT cq.workflow.job.max.procs
(which does not exist as an OSGi property; verified against
com.adobe.granite.workflow.core.WorkflowSessionFactory source).
Factory PID: org.apache.sling.event.jobs.QueueConfiguration
Filename: <factoryPid>-<alias>.cfg.json  (alias is arbitrary; granitewfe is
conventional for the Granite Workflow queue).
Deploy under ui.config/src/main/content/jcr_root/apps/<project>/osgiconfig/config.author/
CAUTION: this overrides the OOB Granite Workflow Queue. You MUST include
every property needed for that queue (name, topics, type, retries, etc.) —
OSGi does not merge factory configs. If you omit queue.topics your override
silently does nothing; if you omit queue.type you get an invalid queue.
queue.name: Granite Workflow Queue
//: Topics the queue consumes. Do NOT change; these are the workflow job topics
produced by the engine.
TOPIC_ROUND_ROBIN is the OOB type for this queue — keeps per-model fairness.
THIS is the parallelism knob. OOB default is 1.
Raise cautiously — higher values mean more lock contention on /var/workflow.
Observed safe range on AEMaaCS author: 1–10 depending on workflow complexity.
Retries before the job lands in the failed state. Matches cq.workflow.job.retry.
Delay between retries (ms).
Priority of the queue's threads.
Keep queue enabled at all times.
